title: Judge dismisses lawsuit of Babson College student mistakenly deported to Honduras
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Federal judge dismissed lawsuit of Babson student Any Lucia Lopez Belloza deported to Honduras due to lack of jurisdiction.
Lopez Belloza missed government flight back to U.S.; judge ruled ICE didn't conceal her location despite rapid deportation.
Lopez Belloza, studying remotely from Honduras, appealed decision; lawyer claims government’s flight offer was a tactic.

title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson called Derrick White a 'top five player' in the NBA, citing unnamed advanced metrics.
White averages 17.4 points, 4.4 rebounds, and 5.7 assists per game, serving as a key player in Tatum's absence.
Boston Celtics won against the Cleveland Cavaliers 109-98, with Tatum and Brown scoring 43 combined points.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum dynamic shines in Celtics win over Cavaliers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: The Boston Celtics defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 109-98 with strong performances from Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum.
Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 23 points, while Jayson Tatum added 20 points despite being in early stages of recovery.
The Celtics' defense was crucial, holding the Cavaliers to a poor shooting performance, particularly in the second quarter.

title: Former Patriots WR cautions against signing Alec Pierce to a hefty contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots seek new top target for QB Drake Maye after parting with Stefon Diggs.
Indianapolis Colts' Alec Pierce led NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025 seasons.
Former Patriot Chris Hogan advises caution on signing Alec Pierce to a $100 million contract.

title: North Kingstown, R.I. police officer dies by suicide, department says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Kingstown Police confirm newly sworn-in officer Brittany Dougal, 29, died by suicide after battling mental illness.
Dougal graduated from Rhode Island Municipal Police Training Academy in December and was recognized for her commitment and promise.
Visitation and funeral services for Dougal will be held in Danvers; donations suggested to local animal shelter or Tunnel to Towers.

title: 9 under-the-radar players for Patriots to consider in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots are considering under-the-radar players like Vikings WR Jalen Nailor and Giants TE Daniel Bellinger in free agency.
Potential free agency targets include EDGE Joseph Ossai of the Bengals and G Daniel Faalele of the Ravens.
LB Devin Bush and EDGE Arden Key, both with past strong performances, are possible additions to strengthen Patriots' defense.

title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady reportedly blocked the Patriots from acquiring Raiders edge rusher Maxx Crosby, now traded to the Ravens.
The Ravens secured Crosby by offering two first-round picks, including the 14th overall selection in the 2026 draft.
Potential friction arises between Brady and Patriots despite his honors, including a dedicated statue and Hall of Fame induction.

title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Colts did not franchise tag Alec Pierce, making him a top free agent wide receiver candidate.
Pierce is expected to earn at least $27 million annually, and Colts are trying to re-sign him.
Patriots seek wide receiver help after releasing Stefon Diggs, linking to Pierce and A.J. Brown.

title: ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Marguerite Flynn, 30, from Hingham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon despite having a rare vascular condition.
Flynn, who struggles with dyslexia and ADHD, values libraries for their educational accessibility and support throughout her life.
She is running in support of the Boston Public Library to highlight its role in providing access to literacy and education.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Amy Comander, 51, from Newton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her patient, Christina.
Christina, a seasoned marathon runner, is currently undergoing treatment for advanced breast cancer while inspiring others.
The race supports the Ellie Fund, which alleviates daily burdens for breast cancer patients like Christina.
